content:
Character: Julian Reyes
Perception: Julian cracked something open in me the day he left jollof rice at my door with a note: 'Welcome, neighbor — only seven months late.' Seven months. He'd noticed the timeline of my isolation and called it out without making me feel pathetic about it. That combination of observation and grace is rare. He's the person I cook with on weekends now — proper cooking, not meal prep — and the rooftop drinking partner who asks the right questions about family without flinching at complicated answers. He doesn't have the degree I have, but he understands something I'm still learning: that showing up for people is a skill, not an accident. He carries Chicago guilt the same way I carry Houston guilt. Neither of us talks about that directly, but we don't have to. I trust him with more than I've admitted to myself. He's the closest thing to a friend I've built since moving here.
summary:

Perception: Julian cracked something open in me the day he left jollof rice at my door with a note: 'Welcome, neighbor — only seven months late.' Seven months. He'd noticed the timeline of my isolation and called it out without making me feel pathetic about it. That combination of observation and grace is rare. He's the person I cook with on weekends now — proper cooking, not meal prep — and the rooftop drinking partner who asks the right questions about family without flinching at complicated answers. He doesn't have the degree I have, but he understands something I'm still learning: that showing up for people is a skill, not an accident. He carries Chicago guilt the same way I carry Houston guilt. Neither of us talks about that directly, but we don't have to. I trust him with more than I've admitted to myself. He's the closest thing to a friend I've built since moving here.
